LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MARK LOVEWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED FROM DEC-30-2008 TO LAW AND MOTION JAN-13-2009, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301 PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES.

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MARK LOVEWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE BEST READING OF SECTION 1304 (E) IS THAT THE PHRASE "PROPOSED FOR CONVERSION" IS INTENDED TO IDENTIFY WHICH PROPERTIES ARE INCLUDED, RATHER THAN CREATING A TEMPORAL LIMIT ON WHICH TENANTS OF THE INCLUDED PROPERTY MAY SUE. THE ALLEGATIONS OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES MAY SURVIVE THE PLEADING STAGE. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. ARIEL M. GERSHON OF THE LAW FIRM OF GREENSTEIN & MCDONALD, TEL. NO. 773-1240 EXT. 351 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED AT 10:00 A.M AND THERE WAS NO APPEARANCE FOR THE DEFENDANT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MARK LOVEWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE BEST READING OF SECTION 1304 (E) IS THAT THE PHRASE "PROPOSED FOR CONVERSION" IS INTENDED TO IDENTIFY WHICH PROPERTIES ARE INCLUDED, RATHER THAN CREATING A TEMPORAL LIMIT ON WHICH TENANTS OF THE INCLUDED PROPERTY MAY SUE. THE ALLEGATIONS OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES MAY SURVIVE THE PLEADING STAGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/ER
